review_text: Nice park. If u need to use the dump point, don't take your caravan around to it...no where to turn your caravan around!!

Very clean and quiet, the toilets were a bit far from where they had put us due to using a tent at a powered site. Great place if you have a caravan but the chalets look very inviting.
Very family friendly.
Stayed here in a caravan
Great location to base yourself and explore the area. Very well kept grounds and amentities, friendly service from all team we encountered. Thanks guys 🙂
Great caravan park. Plenty of room. Amenities block very clean and modern. Park is nice and serene.

Location, service and vibe are nice, and I'd stay again for sure, but I'm withholding a star because the office closes at 4pm and the laundry facilities are sparse with only one dryer in each. When I was there, one was broken so the entire park only had one working dryer that was at the far end of the park. The chalets are nice, but don't have washer/dryer and are very far from both park laundry facilities.
Good location very clean and tidy friendly staff great value for money surrounded by wineries/coffee food outlets
We stayed over Easter and had food stolen from our fridge. The issue was poorly handled and not resolved. If you can’t properly manage the services you offer, it’s better not to provide them at all.
Was nice and clean which is a win and a great little pool. The location is whats amazing right next to the swan valley.

My partner and I came here fresh off spending days in the Nullarbor. We wanted to pay for a nice night at a nice park with nice amenities after our long trip - swan valley seemed like the perfect spot.

The park is very organised, but our tent site was not flat and the closest amenities were over 150m away. Which would normally be fine. However NO TOILET. They had a camp kitchen, dump site, rubbish bins… no toilet… no shower….i don’t understand what I’m paying for? $50 for a not even flat piece of grass? No water on the tent sites, they weren’t even clearly marked we arrived in the dark and realised in the morning we’d parked over two sites so we could be further away from the poor couple next to us.

This park is huge! So many amenities. But NOTHING NEARBY THE TENT SITES? And they had booked us directly next door to another person while leaving two other site unattended? Maybe they’re not so organised?

Quite disappointed to be honest. The bathroom were really nice - however, there are no unisex bathrooms and I need help showering so I of course had to sneak into the male bathrooms so my partner could help me shower.

Thought that for $50 they would have ample amenities for us to use/access. Nope.

They clearly value the cabins and expensive bookings a looooot more…

Even having issues putting this review up as network only running on about half to one bar and park wifi runs on one to two bar with low band width - get booted off wifi every 12 mins or less - even after school hols

This is common in the gday Perth parks but worst here so far

spent 3 weeks on double slab in amongst friendly perms.
great spot if you’re travelling with dogs, paddock out the back and river parks are terrific.
Guildford is has a lot of history, shire has done a great job with parks and maintaining historic places.
Whitman park is a must.
staff at the park to a terrific job.
Planes are noisy during the day but quiet at night.
recommended.
As always, great to shop there.
One of the best places that have visited

Everything about this park was just perfect!! We stayed on an ensuite site with our caravan. Fantastic layout and the staff were so friendly. Lovely location. We enjoyed our stay immensely and we will definitely be back. It was the perfect shift worker weekend 😁 THANKYOU!

Booked for two night and checked out after one. The unit was riddled with ants and had a terrible smell.

Wouldn’t recommend.

- The Wi‑Fi is extremely slow — even at its fastest, it only reaches around 1 Mbps.
- The photos showed a dishwasher, but there isn’t one in the actual room.
- There are only two washing machines that accept credit cards. The other six are cash‑only, and some of them are out of order.
- There are many ants appearing in the room.
- Even when I close the shower-room door tightly and point the showerhead toward the wall, water still leaks out and floods the bathroom floor.
- The staff were very friendly, so I added one extra star for that.

We stayed in a superior 2 bedroom access cabin and it was comfortable enough.
Unfortunately the cabin appeared not to have been cleaned between guests and the floor was filthy.
The mop head fell off on first use due to rot and we replaced it so we could clean.
Bathroom floor flooded behind toilet each time we showered.
No-one polices the 8pm-8am quiet time and the children yahooing around our cabin on electric scooters bothered my mother, who has advanced dementia and is noise sensitive.
In saying all that the staff were friendly.
Laundry facilities were easy to use but would be great if there was more than one dryer and card payment machine.

I do feel the rate to be a little high for the accomodation provided.

We’ve stayed here multiple times and we’ve loved it while we’ve been in Perth. We found the staff really friendly and welcoming and the residents really nice to chat with. We’ve usually camped at the camping spots near the camp kitchen which are nice and open for our Starlink and close to the kitchen is a bonus. Not too far to walk to the loo either. The pool is on the smaller side but maintained good and generally quiet (unless school holidays then it does get packed). The sites are pretty big, we never felt like we were on top of our neighbours at all and we’ve stayed at many of the sites now.
Overall a 5 star caravan park :)

The cabin was lovely and clean (except for a few finer details). We were in a pet friendly cabin with our two pugs. The staff had turned aircon on for our arrival, which was wonderful as it was a very hot day (40°). Pool was great and our granddaughters loved it. All in all a great stay. We will be back
Wouldn't stay here again

Unfortunately, we did not have a good stay. We paid for the most expensive cabin (deluxe) and were very disappointed with the condition and comfort provided.

The cabin only had one air-conditioning unit located in the living area, which was completely insufficient to cool the bedrooms during peak summer. My son did not even have a fan in his room and was extremely uncomfortable throughout our stay.

There was also a significant pest issue. Ants were in our food (resulting in wasted groceries), in our bed, and all over our toothbrushes, along with cockroaches present in the cabin. This was very unpleasant and unhygienic.

Cleanliness was also below an acceptable standard. We found a meat pie wrapper and an apple core under the couch, which indicates the cabin had not been properly cleaned prior to our arrival.

For a cost of over $300 per night, we expected a much higher standard. Additionally, the cabin was only serviced once in a 10-day stay, which further added to our disappointment.

Overall, the experience was uncomfortable and well below expectations. Unfortunately, we will not be returning.

We spent Christmas at Discovery Parks Swan Valley with family visiting from the eastern states, and it was honestly the perfect choice.

The park was clean, well-maintained, and had a great relaxed atmosphere -  ideal for a family stay over the festive period.

The cabins were comfortable and well set up, and the facilities made it easy for everyone, from kids to adults, to enjoy themselves without feeling crowded or stressed.

The location is fantastic too, being close to the Swan Valley wineries and attractions, but still peaceful enough to feel like a proper getaway. Our visiting family loved it, and it made Christmas together feel extra special.

A great spot for families, group stays, and holiday catch-ups. We have actually already booked abother 2 nights and would recommend it to anyone looking for a laid-back, family-friendly place to stay in WA.

Stayed at Discovery Parks for holiday and definitely recommend. The staff were friendly and really helpful. The site was easy to navigate and clean and the pool was great fun. The unit was clean and spacious and has everything we needed. Great location to get places and good parking. Quiet at night too! Great place for family holiday. Really enjoyed stay and wish it has been longer.

We've known this caravan park for a few years now. Unfortunately, there are more negatives than positives. The mattress and chairs are uncomfortable, and the internet in the cabin is unusable. There's also no Telstra reception in the building, so you have to go outside to make a call. The garbage containers between the cabins smell very strong because they haven't been emptied in a long time. Finally, the price is exorbitant; even as a G'day member, it's almost $900 for a three nights stay.
Friendly staff and clean amenities. Great location close to wineries and restaurants.

It was new and clean. Totally satisfied with the cabin 401. There are cooking utensils, fridge, stove, oven and microwave.

Families with young kids might want to move the knives to higher level.

Otherwise all is good.

Highly recommend to get a cabin for the experience.
Toller Standort für WoMo vor unserer Rückgabe, letzter Tag. Kleiner Pool, Sanitär Anlagen alles sehr sauber. Waren abends noch in Nachbar Weingut bei Live Musik.
Great size sites

The location is good and the camp kitchen has most things that youll need.  Like a fridge and freezer, microwave, bbq and stove. There are also charging ports in the camp kitchen, so you dont need a powered site if you dont want one. The toilets are clean and there is a pool for the sites vistors.

Its our seventh time taking our caravan to this caravan park.  We absolutely love getting away for 3 days or so and enjoying the Swan Valley.  The facilities at the park ie pool and ablution are also maintained well.  We will be back

Couldn’t have chosen a better place for a stay. The rooms were of a very high standard. Super clean, fresh and very comfortable. Staff were extremely friendly and went out of their way for me. The location is in a peaceful area, but not far from the shops and restaurants, with a french bakery literally next door that does great coffee and food. Undercover parking and fast wifi too. Definitely recommend!

Not the cheapest caravan park in Perth, but the facilities are well maintained, access is reasonably easy, reasonably central to Perth Metro Area, the staff are friendly & helpful. The only thing that lets it down is the G'Day Parks App, which is not the most user friendly or intuitive app I've ever used, & seems determined to foul up your booking.

Very secluded and peaceful

Nice place to have “alone time”
Always happy with my stays at swan valley. So easy to get to airport. Love it here so quite. Styuff is very welcoming.

I can’t recommend this caravan park. They advertised playground is closed when it should be opened, the advertised pool is generally closed and the WiFi does not work.
When bringing this to the attention of the reception personal, they get unfriendly and rude.

One should expect better from a Discovery Park – this is the worst one I visited yet.

The site was the best site I visited over the past week staying at 4 sites that were scoring 4.5 and above. I do not understand how this site is now only 4.1, perhaps due to historical factors. But for sure, my stay was awesome, ensuite toilets well stocked with good amenities and the place was quiet and conducive for children. I was briefed to drive only at 5km/ hr for the kids safety.

The site was spacious, every parks were spaced away from each other and the entire place was quiet at the evening.

Maybe just one thing they need to work on is to extend the wifi signal to the farthest ends, that’s the only smallest gap
We here in our caravan. Excellent park, very clean, great location & very helpful staff.

We have been G'Day Parks members for many years and been coming to Swan Valley Discovery Park for a few years regularly now as we use it as a stopover for fly in and fly out days because of the location and amenities. We have tried all the different accommodation styles and  locations within the park so we very familiar with what we want when we book. We realise that you can not always be guaranteed that you will get what you ask for which is why alternatives are provided in a polite online request. Several times before we politely requested certain accommodation styles or alternative for stay well in advance with online booking, which should have been easy to manage due to the timeframe but unfortunately not, which was frustrating but do-able at the time. Recently we had a very special occasion coming up with elderly parents we have not seen for a very long time coming from over east for a visit, so we knew the cabin and location we wanted to book due to several comfort factors taken into consideration. To make sure we that we get the specific cabin or alternative for the dates we wanted it we decided to book over the phone way in advance to make sure the number was confirmed. Luck was on our side and the cabin we wanted was available for our dates so we got confirmation on our first choice for dates we had. However on the day of arrival we were advised that people in the cabin we were confirmed to be booked in decided to extend so we were moved to a different cabin which was opposite to what we requested? When we requested an explanation we were advised that no booking preference is ever guaranteed, this even after we were verbally confirmed over the phone the dates and number of cabin? When we asked why the people whom wanted to extend could not be moved to another cabin as we have booked and confirmed this cabin months in advance we were advised that it would have been an extra cleaning fee so it was easier to just move our booking? When we said that we just needed a moment to decide if we wanted the cabin now booked in, we were rudely told with a smirk on the face, upon return by one of the staff that she had already sold the cabin again? We advised we never cancelled the booking upon which she flung  the keys over to my partner, which was demeaning to say the least. We alternatively ended up having to book the elderly parents into another establishment, seperate from us, which was an extra expense and expensive due to short notice, but which provided all the comfort reasons we booked the original cabin. Had we known this prior would we have cancelled the booking in advance and booked in somewhere else more suitable to our needs. So what should have been a very special and memorable occasion ended up being memorable for different reasons all because of an extra cleaning fee, which we would have gladly paid. I hope that management and reception realise what a huge impact a simple request not met and in way that it was not met can have on making an experience memorable in a positive or negative way. There is no reason to make a paying guest feel degraded because of a simple request just because you feel entitled due to your control of the keys.
Booked a chalet for four people . Room was not cleaned properly,doors wouldn't open or shut . Needs a revamp
Loved it here so quiet.

Siamo stati due giorni con il nostro campervan
Molto ordinato e pulito
Servizi di grande qualità

Campsite review: very dense layout. amenities are stingy, we were just lucky to get a spot right in front of the washrooms. stricter on checkout time compared to other campgrounds. Definitely overpriced.

If you want any sleep past 7am AVOID!! Thier campervan sites are directly on the kids playground, within 2m of your site (Terrible Planning). Everyone loves kids playing, but not every morning at the crack of dawn. Parents leaving kids unsupervised to go and play/scream next to someones else home/site, is unresponsible and unfair on other guests. The park should do more to ensure all guests have a relaxing restful stay, but this doesnt seem to be the case. WOULD NOT RECOMMEMD!!
Great place, quiet, clean, friendly and helpful staff.

We booked a 2 bedrooms cabin. When we first arrived, the master bedroom had a rather strong weird old rag odour. Went back to reception and mentioned this, but both staff said rooms were fully booked and there wasn’t anything they could do about it. Thankfully there was another staff at the back who offered to help, and came to the room to use anti-odour spray. Though it helped, smell persisted, and the odour was so bad we ended up not using the room at all.
Other than the odour, everything else was fine. Location was great. There was a small playground and a very small pool within the estate.
An older park. Very clean and tidy. Good value
Great sized Powered Sites, Quiet location, Amenities building was neat and clean with a bath for small children. Very family friendly park within easy drive to Whiteman park.

Great spot within the Swan Valley.  Pet friendly,  clean rooms, good facilities.

When we arrived our room wasn't ready but the staff were apologetic and got the room nice and sparkly!!

Pets allowed which was a bonus.

Will come again thank you!!
Good amenities. Tidy park.
Split system filters need to cleaned, very dusty and it hasn't been cleaned for a while.
Can't comment on room as stayed in own van. Amenities clean tidy. Centrel to alot of the tourist place
Set up 2 camper trailers here, staff were polite and helpful. Sites are well maintained with water, grey water and power access. Pet friendly.

We stayed in a 2BR Cabin (room 209) and it was old and poorly maintained. Room with bunk bed was very dusty, we were sneezing whenever we entered. There's no water mixer in the toilet so water was either too cold or too hot - we had to manually mix the water and bathe from a tub. Not recommended to stay with children.

Toilets had lots of black/brown splotches across the door frame.

2 stars for the convenient location
Massive thank you to Martin, Mr Herne Bay, for being so so helpful late in the day in our time of need and sorting us a spot! Great customer service, lovely place :)
A great place
Toilet block far from camp sites in middle of residential cabins, tent sites sloped, weird dude walking up to car taking photos.
Just visiting  family

My only complaint is the abysmal Wi-Fi connection, I’ve stayed here twice and both times the Wi-Fi is all over the place, sometimes it’s fine, then it disconnects, refuses to reconnect, and then can’t decide whether to work or not.

We don't like caravan parks at all and have very simple requests - a level concrete slab, reliable electricity, water and waste; no neighbours making noise or burning toasts next to us. All requirements were met.
Only a few van sites, very helpful, friendly staff. Very clean facilities. Great position near the vineyards.
Highly Recommend this caravan park Staff is so friendly & helpful,  park clean & tidy...it is a beautiful park.  We will be back

Splendido campeggio con spazi aperti e prati curati. Piazzole ampie e accoglienti. Bagni puliti e ariosi con docce spaziose. Servizio cordiale. Prezzi normali.
Nelle immediate vicinanze una buona cantina...

Booked a powered caravan site . On the booking site stated were a big rig in length . |Arrived and directed at office to site . Wove our way through all the units up a hill to site area . No turn around not able to back van into site as was at the dead end had to reverse all the way back down steep road to front entry then reverse all the way back again to enter site . No longer a van park all about the units . Wifi and phone service useless others complaining at office as well . Toilets blocked off as they were painting the access paths  to them
The cabin was clean and nice, very spacious as well. It was very near to places like Swan Valley Cuddly Farm

Unfortunately the pool is empty due to maintenance. I know it's winter, but as its the last two days of our trip, our daughters were hoping for a swimming pool. We have booked an ensuite: very clean!!
But between all the permanents its not a very social place to stay. We dont come across other Campers like we did in Yanchep last night. But good location for visiting Caversham Wildlife Park and leaving for Airport.
Nice campground with new facilities, quiet and clean. City is easy to reach, bus stop very close, trip to CBD 30min.

These rooms are immaculate! Having a one night "staycation" and thought we'd try Swan Valley. Cannot fault the rooms and the grounds are obviously well maintained. Highly recommended and I will be telling all my friends about this wonderful park.

Head and shoulders above competitive accomodation at this price point. My first time here and very impressed. I'm visiting for several weeks and needed something affordable that ticks the boxes and Discovery Parks onsite cabins could not fault them, nearby there is great spots for a coffeeand brunch or a nice evening meal for a treat. We cooked alot at the cabin but it was nice once in a while to venture out. I'm super happy with this place well maintained really clean lots of grass and. . . . You can take your dog too.

Really enjoyed our recent stay. Big concrete site for our decent sized caravan, ensuite was great. Stunning views over grapevines at the back of our site. A lot of the ensuite sites have vineyard views. Bought our little dog. Location is convenient within the heart of the Swan Valley. Pinelli Wines over the road are fantastic and we visited the cellar door a couple of times. Looks forward to coming back again and again, hopefully the weather is better next time!
Good good good good good

Staff are very welcoming,  friendly and very helpful. Thank you!
Love staying here, so clean and fresh in the spacious cabins, separate, bedroom, bathroom and toilet...everything you need , for one night or several nights. Great value.
Not forgetting,  beautiful white and crisp linen..
Very comfortable for our stay..
Looking forward to our next stay.
Busy road with lots of noise but overall nice enough and good value

Good experience staying in a cabin for young families. However, there is no washing machine and dryers in the cabin. Depending on where the cabin is, a short walk is required. The washing machines mostly accept coins. Unfortunately, the only machine that accept card payment was not functioning. We had to wait a night to exchange coins with the reception in the next morning. Reception is only opened from 9am to 5pm. Otherwise, location is good for traveling around Swan Valley.
Lovely stay close to everything at a great price . Looking forward to coming back soon .
Went during winter and did not use any of their amenities. Decent place for rest at Swan Valley

The campervan parking is side by side like a parking lot. Okay for temporary overnight stop-by but won’t have any privacy and no shower. Okay for late check-in, but do call in to receive further instructions.

Booked a two bedroom pet friendly cabin weeks before arrival, paid and received confirmation email and receipt. After a four hour trip to get there was told there was no booking and no two bedroom cabins left.|Was asked if I rung to confirm my booking which I asked why would I when I received confirmation. Asked how many people were travelling, there was two, so they said well we have one studio pet friendly cabin so that will suit you. Given that we booked a two bedroom cabin this was just a rude comment.|I was then told that sometimes it can happen when two people book at the same time online, but at the same time was told it had never happened before. Either way, you took my money and gave me a confirmation.|I was then told a few days later that the system had somehow cancelled the booking two days after it was confirmed, but no notification or money was given back.
Nice and close to everything where we wanted to go.
Nice, clean and tidy unit we stayed in. The internet was awful and nothing on the TV worked but otherwise great unit
Great place to stay

We stayed here in three separate units as our family travelled from the north west. |The location to the Swan Valley, Guildford, Midland, Perth airport was great. |What we found disappointing during our stay was the lack of customer service at reception and around the park. Staff didn’t acknowledge us as we walked the park or used the playground. It was not friendly at all especially to non-English speaking guests. |We stayed for 7 nights and our linen/towels weren’t changed once. |There needs to be better maintenance of the units. For the price we paid for the week, it was not worth it. The bathroom door squeaked so much that we had to put butter on the hinges!! |There are laundry rooms around the park and washing machines cost $4 per load. We didn’t use the dryers as the weather was fine. |Be careful with your little kids around the park as some lunatics like to disobey speed limits and one way signs.

Beautiful little community, when we arrived past check in we were surprised to have help getting our key and info packet. The cabin we stayed in was clean and I'm obsessed with the mattress. Ac in both the main room and bedroom, goodgooded shower and amazing water pressure. Bodywash, lotion, shampoo and conditioner actually are alright, rare from accommodation in general. Beautiful location, we can't wait to be back!

We had a motorhome site and 2 bedroom  cabin- both were great! The site was a good size and had a concrete pad with some grass. Amenities were great and clean. The cabin was very nice and had a big shower,bathroom with separate toilet- even had an oven. Staff were very nice and helpful- even when i stuffed up dates. Swan valley is a beautiful suburb not far from anything.
Deluxe room was amazing and a great spot to stay for our sons wedding at caversham house.

Our daughter in law made a group booking for the weekend for my husband and I and our four adult children and some of their family. We had a booking for a powered campsite. On arrival the lady at reception was most unhelpful. I questioned the location of the rest of our group. She refused to give me any information. I questioned the location of the toilets, and there were none anywhere near the camp site. I told her that wasn’t suitable for us. She said she couldn’t do anything about that. She gave no further information just handed me a map to find the campsite. The campsite was totally unsuitable, too far from amenities and sandy and unappealing. We ended up camping with our son on the ensuite site that he had booked. The park layout is totally unsuitable for tents unless you are happy to have long walks during the night. I have given a fairly decent rating because despite not using the campsite booked, and the unhelpful reception lady our family enjoyed the weekend.

A well run, clean and tidy park. Great amenities. Pool area is really nice. Plenty of hot water and clean showers etc.||The park only has a few caravan sites with power and water, (and a small number with ensuite) the rest is chalets (many for permanent residents and lots for workers). Sites are a good size. Plenty of trees and well kept gardens. ||You will hear aircraft noise, but you do get used to it quite quickly. ||Terrific location if you want to spend time on the Swan Valley with everything just a short drive away. Plenty of nearby places to eat and sample wine, beer and spirits.  Sadly, The Swan Valley does not have many impressive free picnic places, but there are plenty nearby along the river bank, and of course Whiteman Park is only a short drive away. Medium sized shopping centre 5 min drive has everything you need. Midland Gate is the nearest big centre, about 10 mins away.

Weekend ruined, booked a site grass one got there it was a work site had to leave and head home credit to them we got a refund but do better and advise what you are giving people we would not have bothered.  Pic of grass green sites but complete rubble and sand with view of more rubble.
A lovely park with large shady sites. The toilets near the pool were ordinary and need better ventilation.

Situated right in the heart of the wine valley.
Great position. Clean and well maintained with permanents living there, however very quiet and respectful.
Friendly staff with an easy check in.
Great winery across the road is a must do for lunch.

Si vous optez pour un site « unpowered », vous ferez bien ne pas être trop grand ou d’avoir des équipements qui se déploient… 4m10 de largeur, pas plus, et vous n’avez pas intérêt à dépasser parce que sinon vous vous assoirez sur les genoux des voisins !
Personne ne vous explique de quel côté du panneau il faut se mettre, chacun se met donc n’importe comment et c’est du grand n’importe quoi.
Comme par hasard, le bitume et la pelouse s’arrêtent aux emplacement unpowered… donc vous êtes dans le sable et la terre.
Les amenities sont très loin, donc j’espère que vous n’aurez pas à vous lever pour aller aux toilettes la nuit, ça peut être long !
Par contre c’est près des mobilhomes tout équipés qui nˆen ont pas besoin… allez comprendre !
Seuls points positifs : la camp kitchen plutot bien equipée, l'emplacement par rapport à Perth et le prix (37$/nuit)
On ne reviendra tout de meme pas
Right in the centre of Swan Valley which gave us great access to all the areas we wanted to visit, shame about the weather but can imagine this place is incredible in Summer.
Vaughan and the team do a great job running a very tidy campground. Enjoyed our short stay in a handy location- recommend a short drive to Guildford for food options too.

The quality of the 2 bedroom chalet was amazing. Very few park chalets meet the quality of this one door close and lock properly bed sheets were fresh clean not itchy. No marks holes or scratches anywhere. Oven clean. No rust stains. Secure windows great proper working air-conditioning. Great service. People staying were so friendly and well behaved. Honestly great location lots of food nearby and wonderful facilities. These guys deserve 5 stat's. Thanks will be back soon

What a beautiful place.
The ladies at reception were so kind and sweet. Always putting in 110% when we needed assistance.  Housekeeping were super friendly and bubbly and were super prompt when we needed new sheets.
A massive shout out to Vaughan the park maintaince guru, we had a loose tap in the bathroom and he fixed it straight away with a smile.
The playground was great, the pool was clean and overall it was a very idelic place.
Loved our stay.
Fine for the night, the outdoor bbq was cool.
I have stayed here multiple times. Definitely needs better maintenance on units  but it is a great location with so much to offer only a short drive away

My family and I have stayed at many G'DAY parks, and this is the first time I've ever written a review. If you're wanting to use the unpowered "grassed" camp sites I wouldn't recommend it right now. They're in the middle of renovating the sites at the moment, so the unpowered sites are super small and its a pretty far walk to the amenities. Plus it's on hard rock dirt - if you don't have screw in tent pegs you will definitely bend or break your pegs. BUT it definitely has the potential to be a great place to camp once the renovating is finished. Give it a month or 2 and it will be amazing once it's done.

I have read the other reviews, and yes, there are a lot of retirees living here, but if you give them the time of day, they're really lovely people to chat to. We haven't come across one person who wasn't friendly.

Now, to the reason why my review is high even tho our accommodation wasn't the greatest. All the staff are very friendly but 2 people in particular stood out the most for us, which was Vaughn and Amanda. A massive thank you to use both. You guys are the best. We've had a few hiccups with our accommodation, and Vaughn (the caretaker) was there every time we needed something done. He's someone that would go above and beyond for anyone, whether you're in a tent or a cabin. You both have made my family, and I feel so welcome and grateful that we had someone we could rely on to get things done in a timely manner.

So, it is 100% great service for us, and we will definitely be back in the future once all the renovations are done.
Besides the weather in perth, this place was an absolute banger! Serene environment, built in beautiful pool in park I mean everything was just prestigious.

Stayed on a wonderful, large, quiet, ensuite powered site.
Lovely views at the back of the van. Really nice, helpful managers & staff.
Close to wineries & all the Swan Valley has to offer.
Highly recommend.
Great location near shops and wineries. Unfortunately lots of road noise at night and airplane noise throughout the day and night. Lovely park, friendly staff.
Great place to stay and relax

Must let other campers know, if your tenting and expecting grass please don't. The campsite is currently concrete absolutely horrible for pegs we broke our mallet :( camp kitchen is not attended to , bins were not emptied once in our 4 day stay , fridge was full of food and milk from January says on sign emptied by staff every Monday but clearly not. The site is  more of an aged care facility with older residents living there and to be frank there homes stink as they had there doors open all day and night which we smelt the horrible smell each time we walked the long distance to facilities. There is no facilities around the camp area for some strange reason,  they are so far away right in the middle of where all the elderly live whom don't even need these facilities , you need toilets and showers over near the camp grounds.  The staff on reception were lovely tho always friendly and so thoughtful.  We won't be staying here again sadly as it really isn't for a camping trip

Stayed here for 4 nights. Clean kitchen and amenities. Camped at unpowered sites with good prices but very small spots, especially if everyone uses rooftop tents. Sites 501 to 504 get cramped. Friendly staff. Amenities are a 5-minute walk from the end of the unpowered sites, which is quite far compared to other caravan parks. Wi-Fi keeps disconnecting but sometimes works well.

As far as discovery parks go this one was lovely
Beautiful back drop to our ensuite, vineyards and beautiful settings..
The only thing was the ensuite basin was way to high for me lol,I'm 5'4 and had to tippy toe so I wouldn't get water everywhere lol...but staff are super friendly and helpful,the park is clean and updated
I definitely recommend this place
Great park, good size sites, with access to lots of lovely wineries, shopping, and attractions. Staff were fantastic and very helpful. Gave us some great recommendations for the local area.

Very disappointed. We booked a grassed site for our caravan over the weekend and ended up on a small, sandy patch with no shade. Ablutions a reasonable walk from campground too. We have stayed here before and had an ensuite site which was lovely. Great location, small but nice pool, clean ablutions and reasonable camp kitchen but site was awful.

The accommodation was perfect for our stay in Perth. Clean facilities and accommodating, friendly service. When our air conditioner stopped working on a hot summer's day, we were transferred to another cabin with working air con in no time.
Thank you Swan Valley Discovery Parks Team!

Well that was a disaster. We stayed for 3 nights. On the first day, the pool was closed. On the second day, there was no running  water throughout the whole park, and the pool was closed for half of the day. On the third day, the pool was closed again. This was all during a heatwave with temperatures over 40 degrees. I had a powered ensuite site for our motorhome, where myself and my 6 and 4 year olds slept. and a 1 bedroom cabin for my teenagers.  The teen girls couldn't shower or cool off in the pool on the second day. There is nowhere for adults to sit to watch kids playing on the playground. We went to the office to get ice cream at 4:10pm after spending the day at the local pool and the office was already closed, despite advertising being open until 5. We weren't told when checking in that the pool was closed, despite my saying we would swim in the pool until the cabin was ready as we arrived early. I was told we would get a tect message when the cabin was ready, by 3:15pm I still hadn't received a message so I called them and was told it was ready.

On the plus side, the location is good. We had lunch at Maison St Honore across the road and bought wine at Pinelli. We spent a lot of time at Swan rec centre as we couldn't swim at the caravan park. It is close to Midland Gate, all of the attractions in the Swan Valley and not far from Outback Splash.  The ensuite at our powered site was clean, even though we couldn't use it from 9am until 3pm when there was no water.

I did fill out their survey and also emailed about my experience but after a week, have not heard back.

We recently stayed in the park on a powered site. Unfortunately we didn’t find reception  either on the phone when enqiring about something or in person when trying to check in helpful at all. They weren’t busy when I was checking in. We definitely did not feel welcome at all.
The short driveway space where you park and go and check in is not good as only minimal space for parking and the back log of vehicles are trying to deal with a very busy road before you can get into the bays for check in.

We had a lovely stay at Discovery Park in Swan Valley, the amenities were really well looked after and cleaned and the park was lovely in general. The staff were amazing, especially Andre and Vaughn, including helping us out when our van broke down! Location is really good for mostly anything you wish to do in Perth and we will definitely stay here again! Thank you so much again, from the Lois and Sophia

I did not drop the phone on her... I was travelling and my phone dropped out as I explained to her when she rang back, she did not tell me that they would look again, she went on to say that maybe my child shouldn't be so irresponsible.... you have only one side of the story

Son left his teddy 🧸 in the cabin, apparently it was "never found" by house keeping 🙄
I called the office and was told by one of the reception staff that maybe my child shouldn't be so irresponsible and leave it behind...

As always there's always an excuse 🙄  just  like all your feedback given

Paid $69 for one night on a grass site, and this is what we got. Our feet were constantly dirty, and there was no shade at all. By 8:30 AM, it was too hot to stay at the site. If you don’t have grass, don’t advertise as if you do. Overpriced.

Anything but “Deluxe”.

Don’t bother spending more on the “deluxe” rooms like we did. They are nothing like deluxe! Near enough all of the deluxe cabins are right on the busiest main road (Benara Road).

The room was a bit smelly on arrival, there were ants all over the outside table where there was sticky residues, also ants constantly all around the kitchen sink. One of the couch cushions had a large wet patch which my son sat on as soon as we arrived, I had to leave it outside to dry out.

The toilet door had some brown filling on it and would not lock at all. The main bedroom door handle was falling off. One of the bedroom lamps smelled badly of burning whenever it was on, didn’t appear safe at all.

We were in a room which sleeps 5. The top bunk bed was not even made, I had to put the fitted sheet on myself and then the other sheets on top, that’s despite the hairs etc still visible on the mattress protector! Should you have to make your own beds when you arrive at a “deluxe” accommodation?!

The aircon also wasn’t working properly as the vents wouldn’t open most of the time. No aircon unit in any of the bedrooms and not even a fan in the 2nd bedroom for the kids.

We were disappointed mostly because we’ve stayed here a few times previously in the “superior rooms” which are cheaper than the deluxe rooms and they’ve been much better.

We did inform reception of the issues but we were only there for 2 nights so told them they should look at them after we left.

Calling these rooms “Deluxe” is frankly a joke.

The location is good however with a nice winery across the road and most places within the swan valley are only a short drive away.

Paid top dollar put in an overflow area. No respite from heat no room to put up my awning. Ppl next to us took up 1/4 of my van park. Am appalled at the services. Rubbish bins full / overflowing constantly. Their idea of cleaning the amenities block was hose it out once a day. Waited 3 days and finally 1 soap dispenser got a little soap. Took 2 days for toilet paper to be replaced. And the bin in the toilets were overflowing as well.
Paid to be on grassed site best was a sandy perch only grass was dead clippings. New kitchen overflowing bin that stank no way of cleaning anything up in there. Stove was out of order BBQs were a sty. Everyone around was complaining for money paid how crap this park was!
Oh and btw it's more of a cabin park full of permanents than a holiday park.
What a welcome to WA.
Not at all recommended!!
A Nice Caravan Park, With Good Clean Facilities. I Highly Recommend Staying Hete.

These guys are next level awesome. Had a small issue with power in our unit, went and told the sweet ladies in the office, before I even got back to our cabin there was a dude already there and had the problem solved in a jiffy. Everyone is so nice and friendly, scenery is beautiful, close to shops and necessities. Would recommend to anyone, especially with a family, plenty for kids to do, even accommodate for pooches. Would give more stars but google won't let me. 👍🤙😁

We stayed here 2 nights in cabin that sleeps 8 we had the most amazing time kids loved it beds were comfy aircon worked amazing the kids loved the swim in the pool we have already booked our next stay everyone is so friendly prices were really good thanks again
We camped at this site, really great having a beer and talking to the friendly locals that also live there. The Manger Andre and his wife were awesome. The egg and bacon baps were the best!
One of the great Caravan Park. Staffs are really helpful and prompt. Very clean and well maintained park.

The very best caravan park in Perth, especially those with dogs. We stayed here few times when we visit Perth from our outback town. its pet friendly and most of dogs are quite and under control from their owners, facilities are newish and staffs are super friendly. Its quiet at night time. The park team understands what customer service means and I was impressed with their hospitality with big smile. They are happy bunch of people with good management lead,  I guess.
We stayed at camping site, caravan site, camper van sites and all very good experiences. Location is very convenient for tourists who want to explore Perth cities, Swan Valley and shopping centres in Midland. We love Yahava cafe (where you can take your dog outside) and free dogs play area at Dogs Breakfast cafe (shh the next door Vets have  dog swimming pool ($10 per hour) and  card tab operating dog wash).
Super clean, nicely spaced out, easy traffic, priced right. Highly recommended. Commend the ladies that are looking after the rooms. Bravo!!!!

It’s been 11 years since I last stayed here. Still feels the same, safe, well-maintained, guests are considerate, staff are friendly and helpful. Stay prefer this place as it’s nestled within trees and the scenic swan valley.
Stayed in a 1 bedroom cabin for 7 nights and then after my trip to Albany came back for another 4 nights in a studio cabin.
I prefer the studio cabin as the furniture is newer and the interior is more spruced up. The utensils are also bigger and more user friendly. ☺️
We stayed here a couple of years back and it was great it seems there is a rougher element staying here nowadays not sure if they have a deal with centrelink to house those struggling.

A few months ago i booked a grass powered site for my caravan.  When i arrived the ladies at reception laughed and said ill be staying in the swamp area.  When i arrived at the spot the area was completely flooded.

I seen a pile of wood chips at the back of the park and asked the reception if they could put some woodchips down around my van so i dont get wet when entering and exiting my van but was told they dont have any woodchips and i should go to Bunnings and buy some myself...

I was then told that they can move me to a better location a few days later which they did.

The park wasn't very pleasant, i had people in tents around me and spending most of the day smoking in their car, there was defiantly no holiday feel about the place.

The park was very noisy, motorbikes speeding down the street all night and in the early morning and getting woken up from the planes.

WIFI was terrible and barely had any reception on my Telstra service.  I dont think i will be staying here again.
Was lovely clean well maintained gardens only unfortunate thing was internet wasn't working inside shalley
Beautiful place, nice amenities. Would definitely stay again
Great spot

It's more of a large conglomerate of permanent units instead of a caravan park.
The staff was friendly and helpful.

Perfect location in the Swan Valley, surrounded by wineries & distilleries, yet only 30 minutes into Perth CBD. The staff are exceptional (particular thankyou to Vaughan & Amanda !) and facilities are clean, neat & tidy. New Managers are giving this park a facelift and we would absolutely stay here again.

Mike & Sam (Great Aussie Odyssey)

We were booked in from the 4th Oct to the 9th. Unfortunately we broke down & had to be towed to the park with our caravan.  We asked to extend our stay or some kind of help. We were refused. They did give us one extra day but was told to make sure we were out by 11.30am. Having no car & no help was becoming very stressful & depressing. On top of it all my husband was injured. We did get help from a gentleman who towed our caravan to our next destination. So discovery park swan valley really need to get some compassion.  Very disappointed.  Also the amenities were very dirty.

We stayed with friends and found the staff not very accommodating. We were unable to camp next to each other despite requesting it several times by email and phone calls prior to our stay. Staff stated this was due to no grass powered sites available that were large enough to fit the camper trailer. There was a vacant site next to us larger then the one allocated to them. The one allocated couldn't fit their anex but the vacant powered grass site next to us would easily fit the camper and anex. This made a challenging stay with 3 kids under 2 between us and having 2 sites allocated either side of the park for no reason at all and no anex to use. The facilities were great and the kids loved the playgrounds. If you like a quiet night I wouldn't recommend staying here. Although everyone was quiet and respectful, all night there is planes taking off close by, trains with horns blaring and multiple sirens. We'll stick to camping at open farms in the future!
We stayed at rear of the park, we have a caravan, great spot.  Would've been better having amenities close by.  Would stay again.
Clean room good kitchen facilities. No internet in the cabin . They need to put internet in the cabin. For internet you have to go out of the cabin and use it.it sucks.
Ok. Its a caravan park

Cleanliness need to improve as there is sands in the room.Found there are few big or small spiders in room and bathroom. Overall we still have a good sleep over the two nights stay. The place is quiet.
We stayed in a pitch with no conventional electricity. The kitchen is right next door, so it's very practical.
Very clean and well equipped kitchen. Laundry on the property. Good value for accommodation in Perth
Very comfortable and tidy cabin with a view of the vineyard next door
Great caravan park with lovely, welcoming staff.  Amenities clean. Showers great.  Good place to stay.

After a pretty stressful three hour drive on a dreadfully windy, wet day; it was a relief to finally arrive at the Swan Valley Discovery Park in Caversham.
The ground and reception staff were welcoming, friendly and helpful; providing me with a well labelled Swan Valley Handy Guide, which included all the information/ details I needed for my stay.
My accommodation, a self-contained little cabin, complete with a balustraded verandah and adjacent parking slab, did not disappoint. It was compact, cosy, comfortable, meticulously clean and very well appointed with everything to make my stay pleasurable. Worthy of mention was the bed. It was sheer luxury and comfort plus!
I enjoyed my stay in a safe, secure and well-appointed park, close to the many facilities and attractions available in the Swan Valley.
Such great service and an amazing setting amongst the vineyards.

Lovely stay handman was brilliant had trouble with kitchen sink tap leaking he came replaced straight away bathroom tap was shocking left a note that we couldn't turn it of so hubby turned tap off to stop wasting water office staff very friendly and helpful

Absolutely THE BEST bolthole, quiet, clean and very friendly staff, comfortable, warm and safe. The best was availability of parking, right outside the door. We had a delightful stay over and wouldn't hesitate to recommend this park to friends and family. Quick bookings good directions to find the park and all amenities available. We will be back! Thank you Vaughan and fellow staff members

My brother little just about got electrocuted because their was a lamp that had a mouse chewed cord and when he plugged the lamp in it blew up the rc switch and blew up everything and if it didn't have a rc switch I would be an only child once again
Have enjoyed our time here staff very friendly and helpful.  Not far to travel to Perth & Freemantle to see things.
Well kept park. Cabin is immaculate, clean, and well presented.  Staff is very helpful and friendly.
We have used the camp site several times now. Great location and lovely staff. The grass pitches are very wet in winter.

To Gday Parks
We are starting our big lap of Australia and left Caversham today.
It is , by far , the worst caravan park we have stayed in .
The powered sites were so small, we were unable to pull out our awning, so when it rained the site become a mud pit.
The bins were full with rubbish blowing everywhere.
The ablution blocks were extremely putrid, even after they were supposedly cleaned.
The camp kitchen was so disgusting I wouldn't even wash my dishes in there, let alone cook anything.
Overall it was a very negative vibe, with caravan sites in amongst all the homeless people.
We would never stay there again and will be telling other travellers to avoid this park. 😡😡

Nice park, lovely staff and the biggest clothes dryer I have ever seen. The sites on the hill are a fair distance from the amenities block unfortunately.
But close to great camp kitchen
Staff are rude an blunt the toilets are barely cleaned ,shower your waiting 5mins to warm up no shower curtains
Beautiful unit,lovely and fresh,nice to see room has been revamped and freshened up. Comfortable,cosy and inviting
Stayed for one night, place was ok for one night. It was a general heater and the rooms didn’t have a heater.
Friendly, everything is okay

Placed down the very back of Park amongst permanent cabins and itinerant homeless area. Smelt terrible as I am sure neighbour peed out the back of his van. Very tight fit even though we advised length of van. Was told there is a high rate of homeless hence this particular area not as ideal at the top side. Camp kitchen only temp and was disgustingly dirty and way over in a paddock. Right nearby was the dump point which again told was temporary. Not a great hygienic solution to plonk it near cam kitchen and beside campers. Paid full price for c&$p.

Left after 1 night of our weekend stay. Booked a powered caravan site for 2 nights.
The site was way too small for a 20ft caravan, it was right on the edge of a massive drop down to bush and wetlands. So we couldn't have our young kids outside the caravan as it was too dangerous.
Toilets were a long walk away over in the main cabin area, too far for our  2 and 4 year old to go every time.
The powered sites were basically on an overflow area at the back of the park

Looks their priority is on the challets, would not recommend camping here with young children
The rest are all permanent residents like a lifestyle village? Not what was advertised at all. Especially for $57 per night there are much better options around for alot cheaper and better facilities especially for families with young kids
A nice quiet caravan park , will recommend and will stay again
Campsites had rubbish on them. Bathrooms very badly maintained and not cleaned showers mostly broken. Absolutely not worth the price they charge.
Great little site, great location. We will be back for sure.
Bent over backwards to help us out after hours thank you so much amazing service great park had a wonderful stay
Friendly helpful staff. Clean comfortable accommodation. Enjoyed our stay and would highly recommend. Special shout out to Viv - thank you!
Close to Airport, great amenities, Staff were lovely and accomidating
Tony and Kris were awesome in helping us today - multiple phone calls with Tony about our van and a couple of calls with Kris who sorted us out with a powered site.  Very much appreciated!
Had a tent site. Was great
Power sites are in my belief too small and it's a bit pricey even though we are in Perth. Amenities and swimming are not really clean.

These chalets are still very new. Great location. Has everything you need. Lovely park. We would definitely stay again. Only negative was there was no mattress protector on the lovely new comfy bed. We thought this very odd and not really hygienic. Hopefully these lovely chalets will be kept well maintained and cared for as we would currently recommend over a motel or Airbnb. It was the best accommodation we had during our travels.

Be advised that the grass caravan sites is all dead grass, grass everywhere, in your van on your chairs, in your bed , it blows everywhere, the park never gets watered, better ask for a concrete site,

We have stayed here many times with no worries camping with our dog and our van in the designated area with the new camp kitchen but this time the grass is dead - it’s like straw clippings so messy and unattractive and would turn muddy quickly if rained
The new camp kitchen which has big letters made of wood - looked lovely when completed , is now cracked and weathered so obviously a lack of maintenance - such a shame

Booked a studio chalet. Great value with all the amenities including toiletries, tea/coffee/hot chocolate, ironing board, extra blankets etc. Bed was super comfy. Loved the double layer blinds. Was a bit concerned driving in to see planes flying overhead but team assured me that I would not need earplugs - they were right. Really lovely park with mature trees, plenty of shade - gardener does a great job keeping things tidy. I like how the accommodation is blended - short stay chalets alongside permanent residents and caravans. Nice to hear happy pets holidaying too. Team at reception were cheerful, welcoming and helpful. Close to city without feeling like your in suburbia. Quiet, peaceful, clean. Thanks for a very pleasant stay.
The cabin was brilliant and staff super friendly and a great location close to food, shopping and all the wineries and craft beer pubs

Don't know what everyone's problem is. Seen people complaining about the size of the pool, brown grass to a cigarette butt on the ground. Your standards are very high. Partner I enjoyed the stay. Cabin was clean to our standard and i have mild ocd. We put up with the bad wifi, just put his phone on hot spot and streamed Netflix with a laptop and hdmi cable to the TV. Very clean linen and towels. With your team having to maintain so many cabins a day obviously one or two things get missed. Don't let the negative comments get you down. Those people must think they're royalty. Loved the atmosphere and love the discovery parks, weve been to busselton. We will be back here again.
Great place to stay. Cabins are lovely.  All staff are really friendly and happy to help and advise 😊.

Room was ok, bit over priced, dead lawn all around our cabin, had to park car opposite to entrance of cabin. Pathetic or no TV reception, didn't bother with the wifi. Cleaners need to be shown how to put bags in a rubbish bin properly. Office staff were friendly.
All good, there could be better pool access and more area to space out comfortably

Lacklustre staff lacking any customer service skills.
Showers that run hot and cold during the time you try and have a shower. Dirty shared amenities block with limited security since no one locks door and door doesn’t close. Not something you really want to do unless it’s a convenient place for what you want. Less time spent in there premises the better. Wasn’t cheap either at $75/night powered site…. Yes I supplied my own accommodation. Could you imagine trying to use even $10/night of electricity?????? Location suited this trip, can’t imagine doing it again
Big campsite. Very clean showers and toilets. Small but clean pool. Great TV room. Kitchen (open) sometimes dirty.. Unpowered site a bit expensive. Noisy because of the planes.
Have stayed here a couple of times now. Friendly reception, convenient location for us and quiet park.

This is a perfect spot if you want to be out of the city but close enough if you need. Surrounded by various vineyards and boutique breweries, this Discovery Park is unique. The staff are incredibly friendly, accomodating and helpful. They provide a wealth of area knowledge and recommendations. We have a dog and two kids and have had an absolute blast at this park. @m5_adventures

Spent 6 days here in 2 bedroom cabin. No phone service inside cabins 😕 clean cabins. Walk to pool which was quiet small not much shade. Bbq areas in silly places none at pool area or playground. Playground backs onto people personal space caravan sites. Sun shines straight into rooms. Clean park few locals bit grumpy with all kids riding scooters and having fun. Wouldn't go back to this one not much to do like other parks not child friendly as much. Young kiwi girl friendly other staff not as much. Store sells no ice nothing just ice creams but is shops 2 min down road. tv in lounge area not best highly recommend 3 bedroom cabins not deluxe

Great place to stay for a holding. The cabin we stayed in was very well maintained and cleaned very thoroughly. They even left tea, coffee and hot chocolate sachets as complimentary items. The draws were well stocked with plenty of utensils, cookware and cutlery.
Fantastic stay here,   clean,   comfortable beds
Lovely shady sites of generous size.
Very clean and centrally located

The website says they won't charge you but then they deduct the amount in full. Within days of making the booking & with over a month before the reserved date they are now charging me hundreds of dollars in cancellation fees. Stay clear of Discovery Parks as they deduct your account even when the website says it wants your card details only to make the reservation & will charge closer to the date. They have also changed their policies so no matter what you have to pay for a cancellation even if it's a year before the date. Dishonest business practices.

What a great park.Terrific location,facilities A1 and lovely staff.Think we were lucky with the best site in the park #54.Beautifully shaded huge site close to amenities.Permanents either side,excellent.Pity couldn't get wi fi with it 20 mts away.

Very good service and accommodation.
Arrived and booked after hours and thanks to Andrew the care taker there he was outstanding.
It's great still being surrounded by trees and being so close to the city  - friendly staff and a good vibe in the park 👍👍
We stayed in a 2 bedroom cabin that was pretty much brand new. The park has everything you need and minutes from all the swan valley’s attractions.

We booked 2 hours ahead for a powered site for our motor home upon arrival we proceeded to our site to find it was amongst several permanent cabins with elderly residents, power tools roaring on either sides of us from their carports/worshops. We felt like we were in a retirement village not a holiday park.
When I spoke to reception to ask for a refund as we were not happy with the site and the other site offered to us was very similar they told us they could not give me a refund, that it was their policy. Eventually they agreed to refunding one night. We moved on to another caravan park out of pocket $74 & very disappointed.

I have recommended discovery parks to my friends but I would not recommend swan valley grass camp site anymore.
Reason: It was very dry ( no green grass at all and no trees ) and there wasn’t anything to protect us against the wind and Sun. it Would have been nice to actually see some green grass an a couple of trees in the area

Arrived after hours. Instructions were sent by text. Had a powered site. Space was small but ok. Park was pretty full, but facilities were clean. Liked that there was hand sanitizer available. Yes, we would stay there again, even though there were 2 cigarette butts to be found on our site. I just put them in the bin.

We enjoyed our camp stay here last school holidays. Kids loved the pool even though it was freezing, pool was clean. Our camp site size was on the smaller side as we had 10 person tent and gazebo but we just managed to make it fit. Nice location to explore Swan Valley, ladies in reception friendly and helpful.

Nice park, nice showers, swimming pool is a bit small.

Unfortunately, the reception staff (blonde, wearing glasses) were very rude and filled with self-importance, so it left a bitter taste from our stay.

I loved staying here. The girls at Reception are very friendly and welcoming and very helpful, the same can be said for the guys when you need to swap a gas bottle. I'll be heading back to this park next year.
I can't say anything about the cabins as I had my own caravan.

Our bathroom smelt like sewage the whole time we were there. With 3 kids we couldn’t shower them in that smell. When handing the keys back and notifying them of the problem they didn’t even apologise and just wanted their keys back. Health and safety will need to review this caravan  park and I have notified them after no appropriate response from the caravan park.
Great place!
Great position in amongst winerys. Quick drive into Perth via highway. Very clean park

I booked a cabin for a 2 night getaway for my family and the location is good, customer service was good and the rooms were clean although we packed up and left a day early due to the fact that the cabins have no wifi indoors and the place has no phone reception indoors, the 5 working TV channels is all we had, no DVD player and no internet to keep the kids entertained. As we were packing up the neighbour staying next door asked us if we had wifi or phone reception so we know we wernt the only ones!

Hopefully management take this on board because no phone or wifi would be a huge deal breaker for many

I had an overnight stay here at Discovery Parks. I checked in after 5 however the process to access the after-hours safe was easily explained in a text message. The outdoor light was left on which I appreciated. The cabin was immaculate and comfy. The bed linen smelt nice and fresh. Nothing bad to report, I would happily stay here again.

Dirty commune showers,SML shower cubicles,large buzzing Power towers (radiation waves that causes cancer).staff at office rude,blond with glasses.askwd for extended time but told no space,when on website U could book more time.wifi signal not very good.not a place to come back too..airport park and woodman point very good 5 stars on clean bathrooms and staff nice and accommodating.

Had a good sized paved site close to amenities. Park well looked after, amenities clean. Plenty of washing machines at $4 a pop. Reception friendly and helpful. Great location with Maison Saint-Honore, Pinelli Estate and Funk Cider within walking distance. Playground on site for the kids. Will definitely return as we enjoyed our stay.
Wi-Fi definitely room for improvement, barely have any signal inside the room. difficult to do travel planning
Great location.

They don't tell you that: you adjacent to the Perth airport flight path; you are next to a major arterial route; the wifi only works 5m from the office; the place is 70% permanent residence and very few caravan sites; we did not get our ensuite services in the 8 nights we were there; we asked for a mop head to be replaced as the existing one was disgusting; the sites are very small for larger vans - the only reason why I gave them a 3 star was because of the staff - they were very very pleasant and 4 stars for location

Lovely services and lovely workers that make your stay lovely. Great place chill out or place stay temporarily if you don't have place live at and are homeless and need place stay temporarily lovely workers and understanding
Great location. Very family friendly. All the staff from management to reception to housekeeping and even the groundskeeper boys are always so friendly & helpful. Rooms are always to high standards.

Clean quiet comfortable well equipped
Near to a nice French pastry cafe as well
Lovely park with friendly staff. Super location in the Swan Valley and close to the beautiful Perth Hills.
Great place to get away to relax.
Nice place for spending night..

The 2 room Cabin was very well organised and self contained it was a delight to stay in, it was very clean and tidy, and the main bedroom in the Cabin was very comfortable and warm. The kitchen has everything for cooking and  we highly recommend the Park, the Staff are very friendly and very helpful...Lovely location.
Excellent customer service, facilities and easy to find location. Totally comfortable and loved it here!

A beautiful caravan park surrounded by vineyards. Had stayed in the one of new 2 bedroom cabins with was under offer for $135 / night. Quality interiors, spacious bed rooms and lounge with separate bathroom and toilet. Ideal place to stay with family.
